stimulus: Researchers wanted to examine how diners' impressions of a restaurant menu may be influenced by other menus they have just seen. The researchers began by showing study participants a menu from a sample restaurant; some participants saw a menu with shorter, simpler dish descriptions, while others saw a menu with longer, more elaborate descriptions. Next, all participants viewed the same second menu from a different restaurant and rated their interest in dining there based on this second menu. Participants who had first seen a menu with shorter descriptions rated their interest in the second restaurant higher than participants who had first seen a menu with longer descriptions. The researchers concluded that impressions of a menu may be affected by ______
stem: Which choice most logically completes the text?

answerOptions.content: the time of day at which the menu is viewed.
the level of description in previously viewed menus.
the number of diners who view the menu.
the length of time spent viewing previous menus.

rationale:
Choice B is correct. The experiment varied only the level of description in the first menu, and that influenced reactions to the second menu — so prior description level affects impressions.

Choice A introduces time of day, never mentioned. Choice C mentions number of diners, not relevant. Choice D mentions duration, not varied in the experiment.
